title: For the Weekend You Have Been Looking Forward To
scripture_text: Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
reflection: Rest is not the absence of work. It is the presence of trust. You are trusting the world to hold itself together for a day.
body:
The week is over. The laptop is closed. The phone is on silent. You are going to do nothing, on purpose, for a couple of days.

The Lord, who rested on the seventh day, is not going to be disappointed in you. He is the one who invented the rest. He is the one who said, it is good, and stopped.

Stop. Sleep late. Read something useless. Walk somewhere green. Eat something good. Be with someone you love, and do not multitask. Be fully there.

You have earned this, in the sense that you have done the work, but you have also earned it in the deeper sense: you are a human being, and human beings were not designed to go forever.

The Lord is in the rest. Receive it.
prayer: Lord, thank You for the weekend. Help me to actually rest. To be here. To be with the people I love. Amen.
